sourceRef: tip:What your Enrollment Manager is actually telling you

slides.headline: Understand Enrollment Manager Signals
slides.body: Learn to interpret what your Enrollment Manager is actually telling you.

slides.headline: Identify Common Phrases
slides.body: Recognize common phrases used by Enrollment Managers to indicate changes in enrollment status.

slides.headline: Understand Thresholds
slides.body: Be aware of specific enrollment thresholds and how they might trigger conversations.
bullets: Be mindful of the COE threshold

slides.headline: Act on Time
slides.body: Act quickly on Enrollment Manager notifications to ensure timely compliance.
bullets: Respond to their requests within 24 hours

slides.headline: Document Actions
slides.body: Document any actions taken on Enrollment Manager communications for tracking and reference.
bullets: Keep a record of all responses
Update your system with new information

slides.headline: Review and Adjust
slides.body: Regularly review and adjust your processes based on ongoing communication with Enrollment Managers.
Discover more tips on reading Enrollment Manager signals. Link in bio.
slides.headline: Read the full post

outline.caption: Learn to interpret what your Enrollment Manager is actually telling you. Identify common phrases, understand thresholds, act on time, document actions, and review and adjust your processes. Discover more tips on reading Enrollment Manager signals. Link in bio.

rubricBreakdown.critique: The weakest slide is Step 3 'Act on Time', as it could benefit from a more compelling bullet point to make the takeaway more impactful.
